preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 80%, with Jordan assigned just 4% to win. That 76-point gap reflects a substantial quality differential. Jordan's underdog position is clear, though a draw remains plausible at 16%.

The W/D/L spread shows Argentina as a heavy favorite, yet the model does not treat the outcome as a near-certainty. A Jordan upset carries minimal probability, but the draw sits between the extremes, suggesting competitive intensity—or at least uncertainty in regulation time. Argentina's win is the dominant outcome by a wide margin.

The model and market are broadly aligned. The market puts Argentina at 83%, modestly higher than the model's 80%, while assigning Jordan a slightly elevated 6% chance compared to the model's 4%. Both sources treat this as an Argentina-favored match with limited upset room. The dashboard makes no claim about which source better reflects true match likelihood.
